description_en: A guidance facility for the nationally designated historic site, Sakuramachi Site, located in Oyabe City, Toyama Prefecture. This complex archaeological site, dating from the Early Jomon to the medieval period, is highly valued for the large number of wooden artifacts and plant remains excavated from its low-lying wetlands. The facility exhibits artifacts such as lacquered combs, pottery, and stone tools, providing an easy-to-understand explanation of Jomon people's life, technology, and spiritual culture. It also features a recreated "Jomon Forest" and reconstructed pit dwellings, allowing visitors to experience the Jomon way of life. The excellent preservation of organic materials like woodwork and basketry makes it a crucial site for Jomon culture research in Japan.
access_public_en: Approx. 20-minute walk from Isurugi Station on the Ainokaze Toyama Railway Line, or a 5-minute walk from the 'Sakuramachi' bus stop on the Oyabe City Bus line.

name_en: Higashiomi City Archaeological Research Center (Koryuji Site Complex)
description_en: This center in Higashiomi, Shiga, conducts research and exhibition of the Koryuji Site Complex, a multi-period site from the Early Jomon to the Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site, it is especially valuable for its numerous Early Jomon pit-dwelling remains and pottery, offering significant academic insights into the dawn of Jomon culture and settled life in the Kinki region. The exhibition room allows visitors to see precious pottery and stone tools unearthed from the site up close, providing a tangible connection to the lives and technologies of ancient people.
access_public_en: From JR Notogawa Station or Ohmi Railway Yokaichi Station, take the 'Chokotto Bus' and get off at the 'Maizobunkazai Center' stop.

description_en: This museum exhibits artifacts from the Yano Site, a nationally designated historic site and one of the largest Jomon period (Late Jomon, c. 3,000 years ago) settlements in western Japan. Discoveries include a circular stone arrangement believed to be for rituals, diverse pottery, stone tools, and clay figurines, offering a glimpse into the spiritual and daily lives of the people. Artifacts indicating exchange with Kyushu and Kinki regions highlight its importance as a key Jomon cultural hub in Shikoku.

name_en: Tokoro Heritage Site Forest (Tokoro Iseki no Mori)
description_en: This facility preserves the Tokoro Ruins, a nationally designated historic site with remains from the Jomon to the Ainu cultural periods. The vast site features reconstructed Jomon pit dwellings, allowing visitors to experience historical changes. The adjacent archaeological center exhibits artifacts from each era. While known for its Okhotsk culture collection, it also has a substantial Jomon period display. It is a leading multi-period site in Japan for understanding the continuous history of ancient peoples adapting to the harsh Okhotsk coast environment.

name_en: Tsugaru City Jomon Dwelling Exhibition Museum (Karuko)
description_en: This is the guidance facility for the Kamegaoka Stone Age Site, a nationally designated historic site famous for the excavation of the "Shakoki-dogu" (goggle-eyed clay figurine). As the center of the Kamegaoka culture, which represents the Late Jomon period, it houses and exhibits numerous valuable artifacts, including elaborate pottery, lacquerware, and clay figurines designated as Important Cultural Properties. The nickname "Karuko" means "wetland" in the Ainu language, reflecting the environment where the site was formed. The museum provides an easy-to-understand introduction to Jomon life and spirituality through dioramas and videos, allowing visitors to experience the high level of cultural achievement in northern Tohoku. It is a key facility for deeply understanding the Kamegaoka site, a component of the "Jomon Prehistoric Sites in Northern Japan" World Heritage Site.
address_en: 59-1 Wakamidori, Kizukuri, Tsugaru City, Aomori Prefecture
access_public_en: From JR Kizukuri Station, take the "Shako-chan Bus" for about 20 minutes and get off at "Karuko".

description_en: This museum is adjacent to the Hashimuregawa Ruins, a nationally designated historic site. The site is extremely important in Japanese archaeological history as the location where the first Jomon period pit-dwelling remains were discovered in 1924. The museum displays a wealth of materials, including dioramas recreating an Early Jomon period settlement and numerous artifacts such as pottery, stone tools, and ornaments excavated from the site. As a complex ruin with layers of human activity from the Jomon to medieval periods, it offers a unique opportunity to learn about the transitions in life and culture over time.

description_en: A nationally designated historic site in Sasebo City, Nagasaki, this cave is crucial for understanding the period from the Paleolithic to the Incipient Jomon era. Its most significant discovery is the 'Toryumon' (bean-pattern) pottery, dated to about 16,000 years ago, making it one of the world's oldest pottery fragments. This finding is vital for studies on the origin of pottery. In addition to Toryumon pottery, linear-relief pottery and stone tools were also found, providing valuable insights into the lives of people during the transition from Paleolithic to Jomon culture. The cave is now protected by a shelter and is open to the public with explanatory panels. Artifacts are displayed at the nearby Fukui Cave Museum.
access_public_en: Approx. 5-minute walk from Senpukuji Station on the Matsuura Railway Nishi-Kyushu Line, or a 3-minute walk from the 'Setogoe' bus stop (Sasebo Bus).
access_car_en: Approx. 10 minutes from the Sasebo-Chuo IC on the Nishi-Kyushu Expressway.

address: 新潟県中魚沼郡津南町大字下船渡乙835

address: 長崎県佐世保市吉井町立石473
description: 国指定史跡・福井洞窟のガイダンス施設。日本で初めて旧石器時代の石器と縄文土器が同じ地層から発見され、時代の連続性を証明した学術的価値が極めて高い遺跡です。最新の映像技術や、近隣の泉福寺洞窟の出土品を含む展示を通じ、日本人のルーツを辿ることができます。

copy: 縄文の夜明けを照らす洞窟
description_en: This is a guidance facility for the Fukui Cave, a nationally designated historic site and a key location for understanding the Incipient Jomon period. The cave is critically important as it was the first place in Japan where Paleolithic stone tools and Jomon pottery (Ryukisenmon pottery) were discovered in the same stratum, shedding light on the transition from the Paleolithic to the Jomon period. The museum uses elaborate replicas and video materials to clearly explain the changes in people's lives over approximately 20,000 years. The exhibition showcasing the discovery of some of Japan's oldest pottery, such as Tsubumon and Ryukisenmon pottery, is a must-see.

address: 兵庫県加古郡播磨町大中1-1-1
description: 国指定史跡・大中（おおなか）遺跡に隣接する日本屈指の考古学拠点。大中遺跡は縄文晩期から弥生にかけての重要遺跡で、博物館では兵庫県全域の出土品を圧倒的な規模で展示しています。発掘・復元・体験が一体となった施設で、播磨の豊かな先史文化を多角的に学べます。

description_en: Located next to the national historic site 'Oonaka Ruins,' this museum allows visitors to learn about the archaeology of Hyogo Prefecture through hands-on exhibits. It houses and displays a diverse collection of artifacts from the Jomon period to modern times.

address: 長野県伊那市荒井3520
description: 国指定史跡・神子柴（みこしば）遺跡の出土品を常設展示。縄文草創期の「最も美しい」と評される大型の磨製石器群や尖頭器などは、石器時代の夜明けを象徴する至宝です。館内では、その驚異的な製作技術や、氷河期から縄文へと移り変わる時代背景を詳しく学ぶことができます。

address: 富山県氷見市大境

description_en: A nationally designated historic site, this cave complex was inhabited from the Middle Jomon period to early modern times. It holds archaeological significance as the first cave site in Japan to undergo full-scale investigation.

address: 岡山県瀬戸内市邑久町尾張
description: 岡山県瀬戸内市に位置する国指定史跡。弥生時代の巨大な貝塚や住居跡が確認されており、中世に至るまで約2000年以上もの間、人々の生活が途切れることなく継続した稀有な遺跡です。瀬戸内海の水上交通の要衝という地形的優位性が、時代を問わず居住地として選ばれ続けた理由であり、西日本の先史・歴史時代を繋ぐ極めて重要な拠点です。

name: 国指定史跡 阿高・黒橋貝塚
address: 熊本県熊本市南区城南町阿高
description: 熊本平野の南部に位置する、縄文時代中期から後期にかけての西日本を代表する大型貝塚です。国指定史跡に指定されており、「阿高式土器」の標式遺跡として知られています。特にベンケイガイを用いた「貝輪（ブレスレット）」が未製品を含め大量に出土しており、当時の高度な加工技術や、九州を中心とした広域的な交易ネットワークの存在を証明する重要な拠点でした。現在は遺跡公園として整備されており、歴史の息吹を間近に感じることができます。

address: 北海道茅部郡森町字森川町292-22
description: 縄文時代後期前半（約4,000年前）の道内最大級の環状列石（ストーンサークル）が発見された国指定史跡です。2003年の高速道路建設中に、駒ヶ岳の噴火による火山灰に埋もれた状態で偶然発見されました。火山灰が「蓋」の役割を果たしたことで、配石遺構が当時のままの姿で極めて良好に保存されていた点が世界的に見ても稀有な事例です。近隣にある森町遺跡発掘調査事務所では、本遺跡から出土した土器や石器が多数展示されています。
